title: Chung's Question on Efficient Generalized Regularity
statement_text: Can good (i.e., slowly-growing) upper bounds be derived for the number of parts in the partition supplied by the clustering regularity lemma? More generally, is there an efficient $(H,F)$-regularity lemma (one where the number of parts in the partition is a slow-growing function of the regularity parameter $1/\varepsilon$) for some pair $(H,F)$ with $F \subsetneq H$?
context_text: Fan Chung [1] proved a regularity lemma for clustering graphs—graphs whose clustering coefficient $c_{K_3,P_2}(G)$ is bounded away from zero—and raised as the first open question whether the clustering property could yield an efficient regularity lemma with a slowly-growing partition size. The authors generalise this to all $(H,F)$-regularity lemmas and answer every instance in the negative.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every pair $F \subsetneq H$ of graphs on $k$ vertices, there exists a graph whose every $\varepsilon$-$(H,F)$-regular partition has order at least $\mathrm{twr}(1/\mathrm{poly}(\varepsilon^{k^k}))$, establishing tower-type lower bounds for all generalized regularity lemmas and answering Chung's question in the negative.
notes: The question is paraphrased from two passages in the introduction; no single labelled environment exists for it. The attribution year is inferred from the 2019 reference [1] (Chung's clustering regularity paper); exact year of [1] is not given in the extracted text.
